trans-unit.source: Exercise: Data preparation (Importing PDF tables into QGIS)
trans-unit.target: Exercise: Data preparation (Importing PDF tables into QGIS)

trans-unit.source: Characteristics of the exercise
trans-unit.target: Characteristics of the exercise

trans-unit.source: Many times in your GIS-career, you will come across data that is not easily accessible, or hard to work with. The objective of this exercise is to understand how to prepare and clean data that is in a PDf-file for usage in a QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Many times in your GIS-career, you will come across data that is not easily accessible, or hard to work with. The objective of this exercise is to understand how to prepare and clean data that is in a PDf-file for usage in a QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: [QGIS Interface](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_interface_wiki.html)
trans-unit.target: [QGIS Interface](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_interface_wiki.html)

trans-unit.source: [Import CSV-files into QGIS](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#text-data-importl)
trans-unit.target: [Import CSV-files into QGIS](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#text-data-importl)

trans-unit.source: [Attribute Table in QGIS](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [Attribute Table in QGIS](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: :::{Topic} Context
trans-unit.target: :::{Topic} Context

trans-unit.source: In 2024, the provinces of Punjab, Sindh, and Balochistan in Pakistan experienced devastating floods due to intense and prolonged rainfall. The flood impact on infrastructure is highly dependent on the building material of houses. To determine the vulnerability of the infrastructure to flood damage, we will use data on the wall material of the houses in each province. The objective is to create a dataset indicating which provinces are more prone to flood damage due to the durability of the material used for buildings. First, we need to extract the data from the PDF-file and save it to a `.csv`- file. Then, we need to clean and prepare the `.csv`-file to remove errors and inconsistencies. Finally, we can import the `.csv`-file and perform an attribute join to attach the information on wall material to a vector-layer containing the polygons of the pakistani provinces.
trans-unit.target: In 2024, the provinces of Punjab, Sindh, and Balochistan in Pakistan experienced devastating floods due to intense and prolonged rainfall. The flood impact on infrastructure is highly dependent on the building material of houses. To determine the vulnerability of the infrastructure to flood damage, we will use data on the wall material of the houses in each province. The objective is to create a dataset indicating which provinces are more prone to flood damage due to the durability of the material used for buildings. First, we need to extract the data from the PDF-file and save it to a `.csv`- file. Then, we need to clean and prepare the `.csv`-file to remove errors and inconsistencies. Finally, we can import the `.csv`-file and perform an attribute join to attach the information on wall material to a vector-layer containing the polygons of the pakistani provinces.

trans-unit.source: Instructions for the Trainers
trans-unit.target: Instructions for the Trainers

trans-unit.source: This exercise makes use of the [tabula.technology tool](tabula.technology), an open source application which let's you easily extract tables from a PDF-file. Tabula requires you to have [Java](https://www.java.com/en/download/) installed on your device.
trans-unit.target: This exercise makes use of the [tabula.technology tool](tabula.technology), an open source application which let's you easily extract tables from a PDF-file. Tabula requires you to have [Java](https://www.java.com/en/download/) installed on your device.

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} __Trainers Corner__
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} __Trainers Corner__

trans-unit.source: Prepare the training
trans-unit.target: Prepare the training

trans-unit.source: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.
trans-unit.target: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.

trans-unit.source: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.
trans-unit.target: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.

trans-unit.source: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.
trans-unit.target: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.

trans-unit.source: Check out [How to do trainings?](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.html#how-to-do-trainings) for some general tips on facilitating trainings.
trans-unit.target: Check out [How to do trainings?](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.html#how-to-do-trainings) for some general tips on facilitating trainings.

trans-unit.source: Conduct the training
trans-unit.target: Conduct the training

trans-unit.source: __Introduction:__
trans-unit.target: __Introduction:__

trans-unit.source: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.
trans-unit.target: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.

trans-unit.source: __Follow-along:__
trans-unit.target: __Follow-along:__

trans-unit.source: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.
trans-unit.target: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.

trans-unit.source: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: __Wrap up:__
trans-unit.target: __Wrap up:__

trans-unit.source: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Leave some time for open questions.
trans-unit.target: Leave some time for open questions.

trans-unit.source: Exercise
trans-unit.target: Exercise

trans-unit.source: Available Data
trans-unit.target: Available Data

trans-unit.source: Download the datasets [here](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/Module_5/Exercise_7/Module_5_Exercise_7_data_cleaning.zip) and unzip them.
trans-unit.target: Download the datasets [here](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/Module_5/Exercise_7/Module_5_Exercise_7_data_cleaning.zip) and unzip them.

trans-unit.source: Dataset
trans-unit.target: Dataset

trans-unit.source: Source
trans-unit.target: Source

trans-unit.source: Description
trans-unit.target: Description

trans-unit.source: Administrative boundaries for pakistan
trans-unit.target: Administrative boundaries for pakistan

trans-unit.source: [HDX](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/cod-ab-pak)
trans-unit.target: [HDX](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/cod-ab-pak)

trans-unit.source: The administrative boundaries (adm0-adm3) for pakistan can be accessed via the humanitarian data exchange. For this exercise, we are interested in the districts (adm2)
trans-unit.target: The administrative boundaries (adm0-adm3) for pakistan can be accessed via the humanitarian data exchange. For this exercise, we are interested in the districts (adm2)

trans-unit.source: Percent distribution of households by material used for walls
trans-unit.target: Percent distribution of households by material used for walls

trans-unit.source: [Pakistan Bureau of Statistics](https://www.pbs.gov.pk/content/statistical-tables-pslm-2019-20)
trans-unit.target: [Pakistan Bureau of Statistics](https://www.pbs.gov.pk/content/statistical-tables-pslm-2019-20)

trans-unit.source: Table 7.7 in the *Pakistan Social and living Standards Measurement Survey (2019-20)* shows the materials used for walls per household in percents.
trans-unit.target: Table 7.7 in the *Pakistan Social and living Standards Measurement Survey (2019-20)* shows the materials used for walls per household in percents.

trans-unit.source: Task 1: Get the data from the PDF file into a CSV file
trans-unit.target: Task 1: Get the data from the PDF file into a CSV file

trans-unit.source: :::{Topic} Context
trans-unit.target: :::{Topic} Context

trans-unit.source: The data on wall materials has been published by the [STATISTICAL BUREAU PAKISTAN] as a table in a pdf-file. The table in the pdf-file cannot be imported into QGIS as is. First, the table in the PDF needs to be converted into a `.csv`-file.
trans-unit.target: The data on wall materials has been published by the [STATISTICAL BUREAU PAKISTAN] as a table in a pdf-file. The table in the pdf-file cannot be imported into QGIS as is. First, the table in the PDF needs to be converted into a `.csv`-file.

trans-unit.source: In your web-browser, go to [Tabula.technology](https://tabula.technology) and download the application.
trans-unit.target: In your web-browser, go to [Tabula.technology](https://tabula.technology) and download the application.

trans-unit.source: :::{admonition} Tabula :type: tldr Tabula is an open source application that let's you extract data tables from PDF-files as `.csv`-files. The `.csv`-files can be imported into excel, QGIS, or other data manipulation programs.
trans-unit.target: :::{admonition} Tabula :type: tldr Tabula is an open source application that let's you extract data tables from PDF-files as `.csv`-files. The `.csv`-files can be imported into excel, QGIS, or other data manipulation programs.

trans-unit.source: The Tabula.technology website with the download links to the left. :::
trans-unit.target: The Tabula.technology website with the download links to the left. :::

trans-unit.source: Unzip the downloaded file into a location of your choosing (e.g., Programs, Desktop, ...).
trans-unit.target: Unzip the downloaded file into a location of your choosing (e.g., Programs, Desktop, ...).

trans-unit.source: Open the folder where you unzipped the file and open the "Tabula" application.
trans-unit.target: Open the folder where you unzipped the file and open the "Tabula" application.

trans-unit.source: A new browser window will open with this address: http://localhost:8080. This is the application. If the browser does not open automatically, you can open the browser and enter this go to this address manually.
trans-unit.target: A new browser window will open with this address: http://localhost:8080. This is the application. If the browser does not open automatically, you can open the browser and enter this go to this address manually.

trans-unit.source: Now let's import the PDF file with the wall types into tabula:
trans-unit.target: Now let's import the PDF file with the wall types into tabula:

trans-unit.source: Click on `Browse` and navigate to the exercise data folder: `...\data\input` and select the PDF "pakistan_wall_type7.7". Click `Open`.
trans-unit.target: Click on `Browse` and navigate to the exercise data folder: `...\data\input` and select the PDF "pakistan_wall_type7.7". Click `Open`.

trans-unit.source: Click `Import` and wait for the PDF to load. Once loaded, it will open automatically.
trans-unit.target: Click `Import` and wait for the PDF to load. Once loaded, it will open automatically.

trans-unit.source: Here we will select the portion of the PDF that contains the data table. Tabula expects a table with one row of headers at the top for each column, followed by the rows with the data. By dragging a rectangle on the PDF, we can create a selection where tabula should look for the data table. Drag a rectangle and adjust the boarders so the table fits as precisely as possible into selection. Make sure to only capture the relevant information. Since the headers in this table has an unconventional formatting, it should be left out so the resulting csv table is easier to adjust. We will add the headers manually once extracted.
trans-unit.target: Here we will select the portion of the PDF that contains the data table. Tabula expects a table with one row of headers at the top for each column, followed by the rows with the data. By dragging a rectangle on the PDF, we can create a selection where tabula should look for the data table. Drag a rectangle and adjust the boarders so the table fits as precisely as possible into selection. Make sure to only capture the relevant information. Since the headers in this table has an unconventional formatting, it should be left out so the resulting csv table is easier to adjust. We will add the headers manually once extracted.

trans-unit.source: Once you are satisfied with the selection, you can click on `Repeat this Selection` to duplicate the selection on the next pages.
trans-unit.target: Once you are satisfied with the selection, you can click on `Repeat this Selection` to duplicate the selection on the next pages.

trans-unit.source: Take a look on the following pages and make sure the table is still fully contained by the selection.
trans-unit.target: Take a look on the following pages and make sure the table is still fully contained by the selection.

trans-unit.source: Click on `Preview & Export Extracted Data` on the top right of the window.
trans-unit.target: Click on `Preview & Export Extracted Data` on the top right of the window.

trans-unit.source: A new window will appear where the data will show up. At first, nothing will be visible. First, click on `Stream` on the left.
trans-unit.target: A new window will appear where the data will show up. At first, nothing will be visible. First, click on `Stream` on the left.

trans-unit.source: The data from the PDF table will appear in the main window. Review the table.
trans-unit.target: The data from the PDF table will appear in the main window. Review the table.

trans-unit.source: Click on `Export`, this will save the `.csv` into your downloads folder.
trans-unit.target: Click on `Export`, this will save the `.csv` into your downloads folder.

trans-unit.source: Move the file to the `/data/interim/`-folder.
trans-unit.target: Move the file to the `/data/interim/`-folder.

trans-unit.source: Congratulations, the data from the PDF has been extracted into a CSV file!
trans-unit.target: Congratulations, the data from the PDF has been extracted into a CSV file!

trans-unit.source: Task 2: Clean the data from errors and unwanted entries
trans-unit.target: Task 2: Clean the data from errors and unwanted entries

trans-unit.source: :::{topic} Context The data has been extracted from the PDF. However, there are a few formatting errors and unwanted information. Before loading the extracted CSV into QGIS, we need to clean the formatting and remove unwanted entries. This can be done in many ways, for example in a spreadsheet editor such as Microsoft Excel or Libreoffice Calc. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{topic} Context The data has been extracted from the PDF. However, there are a few formatting errors and unwanted information. Before loading the extracted CSV into QGIS, we need to clean the formatting and remove unwanted entries. This can be done in many ways, for example in a spreadsheet editor such as Microsoft Excel or Libreoffice Calc. :::

trans-unit.source: Open the extracted CSV file in Excel. It might look like this:
trans-unit.target: Open the extracted CSV file in Excel. It might look like this:

trans-unit.source: Excel does not automatically recognise the comma delimited format. We can fix this by selecting the column A, navigating to `Data` → `Text to Columns`. A new window will open. Leave the settings as they are and click `Ok`.
trans-unit.target: Excel does not automatically recognise the comma delimited format. We can fix this by selecting the column A, navigating to `Data` → `Text to Columns`. A new window will open. Leave the settings as they are and click `Ok`.

trans-unit.source: In the web version of excel, you can fix the columns by selecting column A, navigating to `Data` → `Split Text to Columns`
trans-unit.target: In the web version of excel, you can fix the columns by selecting column A, navigating to `Data` → `Split Text to Columns`

trans-unit.source: The data table should now look like this. It is still missing column headers (red). :::
trans-unit.target: The data table should now look like this. It is still missing column headers (red). :::

trans-unit.source: We have to add the column names back, as we did not extract them with tabular. <kbd>Right-click</kbd> on the first row and select `Insert 1 Row Above`. A new row should appear.
trans-unit.target: We have to add the column names back, as we did not extract them with tabular. <kbd>Right-click</kbd> on the first row and select `Insert 1 Row Above`. A new row should appear.

trans-unit.source: Enter the column headings as they are in the PDF-file:
trans-unit.target: Enter the column headings as they are in the PDF-file:

trans-unit.source: Province & District
trans-unit.target: Province & District

trans-unit.source: Burnt Bricks/Blocks
trans-unit.target: Burnt Bricks/Blocks

trans-unit.source: Mud Bricks/Mud
trans-unit.target: Mud Bricks/Mud

trans-unit.source: Wood
trans-unit.target: Wood

trans-unit.source: Other
trans-unit.target: Other

trans-unit.source: Total
trans-unit.target: Total

trans-unit.source: Now we can format the data into an excel table. This will allow us to apply filters to remove the unwanted entries. Select the columns A to F and navigate to `Home` → `Format as Table` and select a table styling of your choosing.
trans-unit.target: Now we can format the data into an excel table. This will allow us to apply filters to remove the unwanted entries. Select the columns A to F and navigate to `Home` → `Format as Table` and select a table styling of your choosing.

trans-unit.source: Now we have a usable .csv file with the information. However, there are still some unwanted entries and a few formatting mistakes. First, let's remove all the rows with the data on rural and urban distribution. We want to visualise the distribution on district level, so a distinction between urban and rural is not necessary.
trans-unit.target: Now we have a usable .csv file with the information. However, there are still some unwanted entries and a few formatting mistakes. First, let's remove all the rows with the data on rural and urban distribution. We want to visualise the distribution on district level, so a distinction between urban and rural is not necessary.

trans-unit.source: Click on the small arrow next to the "Province & District" column. A dropdown-menu will open.
trans-unit.target: Click on the small arrow next to the "Province & District" column. A dropdown-menu will open.

trans-unit.source: In the dropdown-menu, uncheck the `Select All`-Box and scroll down to check all the entries with the value `Urban` and `Rural`. Also add the entries that have numerical values attached to `Urban` or `Rural`. Click `Apply`.
trans-unit.target: In the dropdown-menu, uncheck the `Select All`-Box and scroll down to check all the entries with the value `Urban` and `Rural`. Also add the entries that have numerical values attached to `Urban` or `Rural`. Click `Apply`.

trans-unit.source: The table should now only show the entries with rural or urban in the "Province & District". Select them all and click on `Remove selected rows`.
trans-unit.target: The table should now only show the entries with rural or urban in the "Province & District". Select them all and click on `Remove selected rows`.

trans-unit.source: Now, we have to remove the filter we applied.
trans-unit.target: Now, we have to remove the filter we applied.

trans-unit.source: Lets go through the resulting table and see if we need to fix more entries. There are a few entries where the the values for the percentages are not formatted correctly. Copy the values from the columns __Burnt Bricks/Blocks__, __Mud Bricks/Mud__, __Wood__, __Other__ one cell to the right and enter the numerical value that mistakenly got added to the __Provinces & District__ column.
trans-unit.target: Lets go through the resulting table and see if we need to fix more entries. There are a few entries where the the values for the percentages are not formatted correctly. Copy the values from the columns __Burnt Bricks/Blocks__, __Mud Bricks/Mud__, __Wood__, __Other__ one cell to the right and enter the numerical value that mistakenly got added to the __Provinces & District__ column.

trans-unit.source: Save the formatted csv file in the input data folder (`.../module_5_ex_7_data_cleansing/data/input/`) under the name `tabula-pakistan_wall_type7.7`.
trans-unit.target: Save the formatted csv file in the input data folder (`.../module_5_ex_7_data_cleansing/data/input/`) under the name `tabula-pakistan_wall_type7.7`.

trans-unit.source: Great! Now we are ready to import the CSV-file into QGIS!
trans-unit.target: Great! Now we are ready to import the CSV-file into QGIS!

trans-unit.source: Task 3: Import the Data into QGIS
trans-unit.target: Task 3: Import the Data into QGIS

trans-unit.source: :::{topic} With the data formatted as a usable CSV-file, we can import it into QGIS and join it with a polygon layer containing the districts (adm2) by using the columns containing the district names in english. However, in this step, we might encounter the problem that the district names are written differently. In this case, we can't simply perform a [attribute join](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_5/en_qgis_spatial_tools.html#spatial-joins) as the attributes need to __match exactly__. To fix this, we have to perform a __fuzzy merge__.
trans-unit.target: :::{topic} With the data formatted as a usable CSV-file, we can import it into QGIS and join it with a polygon layer containing the districts (adm2) by using the columns containing the district names in english. However, in this step, we might encounter the problem that the district names are written differently. In this case, we can't simply perform a [attribute join](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_5/en_qgis_spatial_tools.html#spatial-joins) as the attributes need to __match exactly__. To fix this, we have to perform a __fuzzy merge__.

trans-unit.source: :::{note} Fuzzy merge is a technique used in data processing to combine two datasets based on similar, but not necessarily identical, values. This is particularly useful when dealing with data that may have inconsistencies, such as typos or different formats.
trans-unit.target: :::{note} Fuzzy merge is a technique used in data processing to combine two datasets based on similar, but not necessarily identical, values. This is particularly useful when dealing with data that may have inconsistencies, such as typos or different formats.

trans-unit.source: Instead of looking for exact matches, fuzzy merge uses algorithms to compare values and determine how similar they are. For example, "Jon" and John" might be considered a match because how similar they are. Each comparison generates a similarity score, which indicates how closely the two values match.
trans-unit.target: Instead of looking for exact matches, fuzzy merge uses algorithms to compare values and determine how similar they are. For example, "Jon" and John" might be considered a match because how similar they are. Each comparison generates a similarity score, which indicates how closely the two values match.

trans-unit.source: Open QGIS and create a new project.
trans-unit.target: Open QGIS and create a new project.

trans-unit.source: Save the project in the exercise folder.
trans-unit.target: Save the project in the exercise folder.

trans-unit.source: [Import the CSV layer into QGIS](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#text-data-import).
trans-unit.target: [Import the CSV layer into QGIS](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#text-data-import).

trans-unit.source: Import the administrative boundaries located in the data input folder: `.../module_5_ex_7_data_cleansing/data/input/`
trans-unit.target: Import the administrative boundaries located in the data input folder: `.../module_5_ex_7_data_cleansing/data/input/`

trans-unit.source: Now let's perform a fuzzy merge: Open the [field calculator](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_table_functions_wiki.html#calculate-field) __for the layer called `tabula-pakistan_wall_type7.7`__ and enter the following expression:
trans-unit.target: Now let's perform a fuzzy merge: Open the [field calculator](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_table_functions_wiki.html#calculate-field) __for the layer called `tabula-pakistan_wall_type7.7`__ and enter the following expression:

trans-unit.source: Enter an `Output field name`, set the `Output field type` to `Text (string)` and increase the `output field length` to 40.
trans-unit.target: Enter an `Output field name`, set the `Output field type` to `Text (string)` and increase the `output field length` to 40.

trans-unit.source: Click `OK`. The CSV file should now have a new column. In this column you will find the values from the adm2 polygon layer which have been matched using the fuzzy merge algorithm.
trans-unit.target: Click `OK`. The CSV file should now have a new column. In this column you will find the values from the adm2 polygon layer which have been matched using the fuzzy merge algorithm.

trans-unit.source: We can now perform an attribute join by selecting the `ADM2_EN` and newly created fuzzy merge column as identifying column. In the processing toolbox, search for the tool __"Join attributes by field value"__. <kbd>Double-click</kbd> on it.
trans-unit.target: We can now perform an attribute join by selecting the `ADM2_EN` and newly created fuzzy merge column as identifying column. In the processing toolbox, search for the tool __"Join attributes by field value"__. <kbd>Double-click</kbd> on it.

trans-unit.source: A new window will open. Here, set the following parameters:
trans-unit.target: A new window will open. Here, set the following parameters:

trans-unit.source: __Input layer:__ ADM2 polygon layer
trans-unit.target: __Input layer:__ ADM2 polygon layer

trans-unit.source: __Table field:__ `ADM2_EN`
trans-unit.target: __Table field:__ `ADM2_EN`

trans-unit.source: __Input layer 2:__ `tabula-pakistan_wall_type7.7`
trans-unit.target: __Input layer 2:__ `tabula-pakistan_wall_type7.7`

trans-unit.source: __Table field 2:__ `Fuzzy_match`
trans-unit.target: __Table field 2:__ `Fuzzy_match`

trans-unit.source: __Layer 2 fields to copy:__ `Burnt Bricks/Blocks, Mud Bricks/Mud, Wood, Other`
trans-unit.target: __Layer 2 fields to copy:__ `Burnt Bricks/Blocks, Mud Bricks/Mud, Wood, Other`

trans-unit.source: The "Join attributes by field value" parameters :::
trans-unit.target: The "Join attributes by field value" parameters :::

trans-unit.source: Click `Run`.
trans-unit.target: Click `Run`.

trans-unit.source: Congratulations, we have now successfully joined the information from the PDF file to a polygon layer!
trans-unit.target: Congratulations, we have now successfully joined the information from the PDF file to a polygon layer!

trans-unit.source: Task 4: Visualisation of the Data
trans-unit.target: Task 4: Visualisation of the Data

trans-unit.source: We extracted the data from the PDF and now have a polygon layer containing the information on the wall material per district. With the new column, we can create a map visualising the amount of buildings that have, for example, burnt bricks or blocks as wall material.
trans-unit.target: We extracted the data from the PDF and now have a polygon layer containing the information on the wall material per district. With the new column, we can create a map visualising the amount of buildings that have, for example, burnt bricks or blocks as wall material.

trans-unit.source: Open the layer styling panel and select the __Graduated__ symbolisation method.
trans-unit.target: Open the layer styling panel and select the __Graduated__ symbolisation method.

trans-unit.source: Under `Value`, select `Burnt Bricks/Blocks`.
trans-unit.target: Under `Value`, select `Burnt Bricks/Blocks`.

trans-unit.source: Click on `Classify`.
trans-unit.target: Click on `Classify`.

trans-unit.source: The resulting symbolisation could look something like this:
trans-unit.target: The resulting symbolisation could look something like this:

trans-unit.source: The darker the colour, the higher the percentage of buildings having burnt bricks or blocks as wall type. The grey areas are the districts where we there is no data available. :::
trans-unit.target: The darker the colour, the higher the percentage of buildings having burnt bricks or blocks as wall type. The grey areas are the districts where we there is no data available. :::
